code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test26-AUG-2016Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria and glucosuria, with low specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal tubular function. The presence of ketones and bilirubin in urine, along with abnormal urine appearance and color, further supports renal dysfunction. No evidence of urinary tract infection is seen. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for possible diabetic or other systemic renal disease.

Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
